Name: Anti-Aliasing
Description: Controls anti-aliasing techniques including post-process edge smoothing (FXAA/DLAA) and temporal accumulation. Provides fine control over edge detection, temporal feedback, sharpening, and stability to balance image quality and performance.

Fields.Name: Enable Anti-Alias
Fields.Description: Enables or disables the anti-aliasing system. When disabled, no edge smoothing or temporal accumulation is applied.

Fields.Name: Anti-Alias Type
Fields.Description: Selects the anti-aliasing method. Determines which parameters are active.

Fields.Name: FXAA: Alpha
Fields.Description: Controls blending strength of FXAA edge smoothing. Higher values increase smoothing but may blur fine detail.

Fields.Name: FXAA: Reduce Minimum
Fields.Description: Minimum edge detection threshold for FXAA. Lower values make the filter more sensitive to subtle edges.

Fields.Name: FXAA: Reduce Multiplier
Fields.Description: Multiplier controlling edge detection sensitivity. Higher values reduce over-blurring by limiting weak edge influence.

Fields.Name: FXAA: Span Max
Fields.Description: Maximum sampling span for FXAA edge search. Higher values improve quality on large edges but increase cost.

Fields.Name: DLAA: Threshold
Fields.Description: Edge detection threshold for DLAA. Determines how strong a contrast must be to be treated as an edge.

Fields.Name: DLAA: Smoothing Strength
Fields.Description: Controls smoothing strength in DLAA. Higher values increase blending across detected edges.

Fields.Name: DLAA: Epsilon
Fields.Description: Small bias value to stabilize DLAA edge detection and prevent noise or flickering.
